words.def: connected with managing a business or an organization, and with making plans and decisions
words.example: She has an executive position in a finance company.

words.def: an object or a collection of objects put in a public place, for example a museum, so that people can see it
words.example: The museum contains some interesting exhibits on Spanish rural life.

words.def: the state or fact of being real or living or of being present
words.example: I was unaware of his existence until today.

words.def: from or in another country, especially a tropical one; seeming exciting and unusual because it seems to be connected with foreign countries
words.example: brightly-coloured exotic flowers/plants/birds

words.def: an act of increasing or making something increase in size, amount or importance
words.example: a period of rapid economic expansion

words.def: a belief that something will happen because it is likely
words.example: expectation of something, We are confident in our expectation of a full recovery.

words.def: that you think will happen
words.example: Double the expected number of people came to the meeting.

words.def: an organized journey with a particular purpose, especially to find out about a place that is not well known
words.example: to plan/lead/go on an expedition

words.def: the money that you spend on something
words.example: The garden was transformed at great expense.

words.def: done with, having or involving great knowledge or skill
words.example: to seek expert advice

words.def: expert knowledge or skill in a particular subject, activity or job
words.example: professional/scientific/technical, etc. expertise

words.def: to burst (= break apart) or make something burst loudly and violently, causing damage
words.example: A second bomb exploded in a crowded market.

words.def: to treat a person or situation as an opportunity to gain an advantage for yourself
words.example: He exploited his father's name to get himself a job.

words.def: the act of travelling through a place in order to find out about it or look for something in it
words.example: Budgets for space exploration have been cut back.

words.def: the selling and transporting of goods to another country
words.example: a ban on the export of live cattle

words.def: to show something that is usually hidden
words.example: expose something, He smiled suddenly, exposing a set of amazingly white teeth.

words.def: the state of being in a place or situation where there is no protection from something harmful or unpleasant
words.example: prolonged exposure to harmful radiation

words.def: to make something longer or larger
words.example: to extend a fence/road/house

words.def: the act of increasing the area of activity, group of people, etc. that is affected by something
words.example: the extension of new technology into developing countries

words.def: covering a large area; great in amount
words.example: The house has extensive grounds.

words.def: in a way that covers a large area
words.example: She has travelled extensively.

words.def: how large, important, serious, etc. something is
words.example: It is difficult to assess the full extent of the damage.

words.def: connected with or located on the outside of something/somebody
words.example: the external walls of the building

words.def: unexpected, surprising or strange
words.example: What an extraordinary thing to say!

words.def: very great in degree
words.example: We are working under extreme pressure at the moment.

words.def: material made by weaving wool, cotton, silk, etc., used for making clothes, curtains, etc. and for covering furniture
words.example: They sell a wide variety of printed cotton fabric.

words.def: extremely good
words.example: They put on a fabulous performance.

words.def: buildings, services, equipment, etc. that are provided for a particular purpose
words.example: leisure/sports facilities

words.def: one of several things that cause or influence something
words.example: Obesity is a major risk factor for heart disease.

words.def: not successful
words.example: a failed writer

words.def: lack of success in doing or achieving something
words.example: The success or failure of the plan depends on you.

words.def: trust in somebody’s ability or knowledge; trust that somebody/something will do what has been promised
words.example: If the company can retain its customers' faith, it could become the market leader.

words.def: not what somebody claims it is; appearing to be something it is not
words.example: There were a few stalls selling fake designer clothing.

words.def: the state of being known and talked about by many people
words.example: to achieve/win instant fame

words.def: a pleasant situation that you imagine but that is unlikely to happen
words.example: He spoke of his childhood fantasies about becoming a famous football player.

words.def: at a greater distance away from you
words.example: I saw her on the far side of the road.

words.def: the money that you pay to travel by bus, plane, taxi, etc.
words.example: bus/taxi fares

words.def: the responsibility for something wrong that has happened or been done
words.example: Why should I say sorry when it's not my fault?

words.def: a thing that you do to help somebody
words.example: Could you do me a favour and pick up Sam from school today?

words.def: one of the many soft light parts covering a bird’s body
words.example: a peacock feather

words.def: having a system of government in which the individual states of a country have control over their own affairs, but are controlled by a central government for national decisions, etc.
words.example: a federal republic

words.def: an amount of money that you pay for professional advice or services
words.example: Employees are reimbursed for any legal fees incurred when they relocate.

words.def: a meal of milk for a young baby; a meal for an animal
words.example: her morning feed

words.def: advice, criticism or information about how good or useful something or somebody’s work is
words.example: customer/user feedback

words.def: the feeling you get when you touch something or are touched
words.example: You can tell it's silk by the feel.

words.def: used to describe somebody who is the same as you in some way, or in the same situation
words.example: fellow citizens/students

words.def: a medical condition in which a person has a temperature that is higher than normal
words.example: He has a high fever.

words.def: the activity of being involved in a battle against an enemy
words.example: Fighting broke out in three districts of the city last night.

words.def: a number representing a particular amount, especially one given in official information
words.example: the latest sales/crime/unemployment figures

words.def: a box or folded piece of card for keeping loose papers together and in order
words.example: a box file

words.def: money used to run a business, an activity or a project
words.example: The project will only go ahead if they can raise the necessary finance.

words.def: information that is discovered as the result of research into something
words.example: Our research findings indicate that pregnant women benefit from this treatment.

words.def: a person whose job is to put out fires
words.example: Firefighters were called to a house in Summertown.

words.def: a small device containing powder that burns or explodes and produces bright coloured lights and loud noises, used especially at celebrations
words.example: (British English), to let off a few fireworks

words.def: fairly hard; not easy to press into a different shape
words.example: a firm bed/mattress

words.def: in a strong or definite way
words.example: ‘I can manage,’ she said firmly.

words.def: used to introduce the first of a list of points you want to make in a speech or piece of writing
words.example: There are two reasons for this decision: firstly…

words.def: a solution to a problem, especially an easy or temporary one
words.example: Luckily there is an easy fix.

words.def: staying the same; not changing or able to be changed
words.example: These fixed prices give farmers a degree of financial security.

words.def: a hot bright stream of burning gas that comes from something that is on fire
words.example: the tiny yellow flame of a match

words.def: a sudden bright light that shines for a moment and then disappears
words.example: a flash of lightning

words.def: how food or drink tastes
words.example: The tomatoes give extra flavour to the sauce.

words.def: able to change to suit new conditions or situations
words.example: a more flexible approach

words.def: to move slowly on water or in the air
words.example: A group of swans floated by.

words.def: the thing or person that people are most interested in; the act of paying special attention to something and making people interested in it
words.example: focus of something, It was the main focus of attention at the meeting.

words.def: a part of something, especially cloth, that is folded or hangs as if it had been folded
words.example: the folds of her dress

words.def: that can be folded, so that it can be carried or stored in a small space
words.example: a folding chair/bike/knife

words.def: that is/are going to be mentioned next
words.example: Answer the following questions.

words.def: having warm or loving feelings for somebody, especially somebody you have known for a long time
words.example: Over the years, I have grown quite fond of her.

words.def: a person who you think behaves or speaks in a way that lacks intelligence or good judgement
words.example: Don't be such a fool!

words.def: to order somebody not to do something; to order that something must not be done
words.example: forbid somebody (from doing something), He forbade them from mentioning the subject again.

words.def: a statement about what will happen in the future, based on information that is available now
words.example: The sales forecasts are encouraging.
